sentences.en: In general, codex seems to be moving into the way of hiding as much from the user as possible, and it wouldn't surprise me if they eventually start encrypting the entire session logs just like they did with agent>sub-agent prompts recently.
sentences.zh: 总的来说，Codex 似乎正在转向尽可能向用户隐藏的方式，如果他们最终开始加密整个会话日志，就像他们最近对代理>子代理提示所做的那样，我不会感到惊讶。

sentences.en: Sad though as it's easily the best harness+model combo available today out of the ones I've tried.
sentences.zh: 但很遗憾，因为它无疑是我尝试过的目前最好的安全带+模型组合。

blocks.html_en:
That has nothing to do with context window or the models at all; it's the compute capacity.

Pretty much every single AI firm had the same issue when the new model came out, it's the result of everyone jumping onboard and consuming all available compute capacity to break it for days.

Claude had the same issues when they released their newer models.

sentences.en: That has nothing to do with context window or the models at all; it's the compute capacity.Pretty much every single AI firm had the same issue when the new model came out, it's the result of everyone jumping onboard and consuming all available compute capacity to break it for days.Claude had the same issues when they released their newer models.
sentences.zh: 这与上下文窗口或模型完全无关；这是计算能力。当新模型出现时，几乎每个人工智能公司都遇到了同样的问题，这是每个人都跳上船并消耗所有可用计算能力来打破它好几天的结果。克劳德在发布新模型时也遇到了同样的问题。

blocks.html_en:
> That has nothing to do with context window or the models at all; it's the compute capacity.

Quite literally a harness feature/bug though, that it's unable to compact when reaching the max context (which seemingly both codex and Claude Code struggled with in the beginning), not something that will happen at every model release forever.

Hasn't happen for at least the two last model releases in Codex, not sure why you'd imply that.

sentences.en: > That has nothing to do with context window or the models at all; it's the compute capacity.Quite literally a harness feature/bug though, that it's unable to compact when reaching the max context (which seemingly both codex and Claude Code struggled with in the beginning), not something that will happen at every model release forever.Hasn't happen for at least the two last model releases in Codex, not sure why you'd imply that.
sentences.zh: > 这与上下文窗口或模型完全无关；这是计算能力。实际上，它是一个利用功能/错误，当达到最大上下文时，它无法压缩（这似乎是 Codex 和 Claude Code 在开始时都遇到的问题），而不是在每个模型版本中永远发生的事情。至少在 Codex 的最后两个模型版本中不会发生这种情况，不知道为什么你会暗示这一点。

blocks.html_en:
Claude also had issues with this kind of thing for months early on, too, where it simply couldn't compact its own emissions.

But definitely earlier GPT models suffered greatly as context got large, and the compaction itself in Codex was really crappy. That changed around January.

sentences.en: Claude also had issues with this kind of thing for months early on, too, where it simply couldn't compact its own emissions.But definitely earlier GPT models suffered greatly as context got large, and the compaction itself in Codex was really crappy.
sentences.zh: 早在几个月前，Claude 也遇到了此类问题，它根本无法压缩自己的排放量。但是，随着上下文变大，早期的 GPT 模型肯定会受到很大影响，而且 Codex 中的压缩本身确实很糟糕。

sentences.en: That changed around January.
sentences.zh: 一月份左右情况发生了变化。

blocks.html_en: For me, Codex regularly forgets to complete its last task when compaction occurs, especially when the last message I sent was right before compaction.

blocks.html_en: No matter how good compaction is, on some big projects it needs to read a lot of files. In my experience the first 200.000 tokens go FAST, but after that it slows down. Most of my Fable sessions don't go over 500.000 tokens, I don't need to compact once. But when I use Codex a single session has to compact over and over again.

sentences.en: No matter how good compaction is, on some big projects it needs to read a lot of files.
sentences.zh: 无论压缩多么好，在一些大项目上它都需要读取大量文件。

sentences.en: In my experience the first 200.000 tokens go FAST, but after that it slows down.
sentences.zh: 根据我的经验，前 200,000 个代币速度很快，但之后速度就会变慢。

sentences.en: Most of my Fable sessions don't go over 500.000 tokens, I don't need to compact once.
sentences.zh: 我的大部分 Fable 会话不会超过 500.000 个令牌，我不需要压缩一次。

sentences.en: But when I use Codex a single session has to compact over and over again.
sentences.zh: 但是当我使用 Codex 时，单个会话必须一遍又一遍地压缩。

blocks.html_en: In my experience the only reason it needs to read a lot of files is if your agents.md is deficient. It should only need to read the file it's actually working on, maybe a few others. Everything else should be explained in documentation

sentences.zh: 它应该只需要读取它实际正在处理的文件，也许还有其他一些文件。

sentences.en: Everything else should be explained in documentation
sentences.zh: 其他一切都应该在文档中解释

blocks.html_en:
That's quite small for my workloads.

I try to keep it under 200k but my DeepSeek and MiMo sessions can sometimes grow to 350k tokens when I try to squeeze one last iteration I compact.

Can't OpenAI copy DeepSeek K/V cache tech (from published papers) to make it super cheap?

sentences.en: That's quite small for my workloads.I try to keep it under 200k but my DeepSeek and MiMo sessions can sometimes grow to 350k tokens when I try to squeeze one last iteration I compact.Can't OpenAI copy DeepSeek K/V cache tech (from published papers) to make it super cheap?
sentences.zh: 这对于我的工作负载来说相当小。我尝试将其保持在 200k 以下，但当我尝试压缩压缩的最后一次迭代时，我的 DeepSeek 和 MiMo 会话有时会增长到 350k 令牌。OpenAI 不能复制 DeepSeek K/V 缓存技术（来自已发表的论文）以使其超级便宜吗？

blocks.html_en:
Nobody does caching as well as DeepSeek, so I guess it's a big enough difference in the implementation to make it difficult.

If you use Reasonix with DeepSeek it gets silly, as it is append-only to work with how caching works. It gets something like 97-98% cached tokens in a long session. It makes an already cheap model even cheaper.

sentences.en: Nobody does caching as well as DeepSeek, so I guess it's a big enough difference in the implementation to make it difficult.If you use Reasonix with DeepSeek it gets silly, as it is append-only to work with how caching works.
sentences.zh: 没有人像 DeepSeek 那样进行缓存，所以我猜想这在实现上有足够大的差异，使其变得困难。如果您将 Reasonix 与 DeepSeek 一起使用，它会变得很愚蠢，因为它只是附加来处理缓存的工作方式。

sentences.en: It gets something like 97-98% cached tokens in a long session.
sentences.zh: 它在长时间会话中获得了 97-98% 的缓存令牌。

sentences.en: It makes an already cheap model even cheaper.
sentences.zh: 它使本来就很便宜的型号变得更加便宜。

blocks.html_en:
Nobody does caching as well as DeepSeek, so I guess it's a big enough difference in the implementation to make it difficult.

If you use Reasonix with DeepSeek it gets silly, as it is append-only to work with how caching works. It gets something like 97-98% cached tokens in a long session. It makes an already cheap model even cheaper.

blocks.html_en:
using open models and local AI via llamacpp, I'm compacting (via agent directed) between 55k-85k; going up to 120k is rare unless the agent is actually following a non trivial log tracing or some other thing that absolutely requires keeping large context.

I've also tailored an agent system prompt with llamacpp reasoning budget and message that asks the agent to spawn subagents then compress to reduce context bloat. It's in opencode using the dynamic context pruning, so it's mostly keeping a vector&direction without having to bloat up.

It mostly works to allow iteration into multiple sub components.

sentences.en: using open models and local AI via llamacpp, I'm compacting (via agent directed) between 55k-85k; going up to 120k is rare unless the agent is actually following a non trivial log tracing or some other thing that absolutely requires keeping large context.I've also tailored an agent system prompt with llamacpp reasoning budget and message that asks the agent to spawn subagents then compress to reduce context bloat.
sentences.zh: 通过 llamacpp 使用开放模型和本地 AI，我在 55k-85k 之间压缩（通过代理定向）；达到 120k 的情况很少见，除非代理实际上正在跟踪重要的日志跟踪或其他绝对需要保留大型上下文的事情。我还定制了一个代理系统提示，其中包含 llamacpp 推理预算和消息，要求代理生成子代理，然后进行压缩以减少上下文膨胀。

sentences.en: It's in opencode using the dynamic context pruning, so it's mostly keeping a vector&direction without having to bloat up.It mostly works to allow iteration into multiple sub components.
sentences.zh: 它是使用动态上下文修剪的开放代码，因此它主要保留向量和方向而不必膨胀。它主要用于允许迭代到多个子组件。

blocks.html_en:
On long sessions even with workflows and sub-agents I typically hit around 500-750k tokens in context (say 4-16 hours of work in a session, across multiple 5-hour limits) with Claude Code.

I was going to explore paying for Codex since OpenAI seems to be a bit more generous with rate limits, but I'm now not sure - for the stuff I do limited context size would be a dealbreaker - basically lots and lots of documentation and guideline references, code review loops, documentation references and web searches, multi-repo exploration, lots of tool calls and so on.

I might just upgrade to Anthropic's more expensive Max subscription or something to get more subsidized tokens. Even with plan files and the plan mode, it's like a slot machine after compacting the context, sometimes steps or other details just evaporate in thin air, the less of that I need to do the better. It's not that it can't work, it just doesn't work reliably enough not to be annoying.

Luckily DeepSeek V4 Pro, GLM 5.2 and Kimi K3 don't seem to have those limits either - though DS is around Sonnet, GLM 5.2 feels a bit above Sonnet and only K3 only really is in the proper Opus ballpark that's good enough for me to work undisturbed, even if it seems to be slower.

sentences.en: On long sessions even with workflows and sub-agents I typically hit around 500-750k tokens in context (say 4-16 hours of work in a session, across multiple 5-hour limits) with Claude Code.I was going to explore paying for Codex since OpenAI seems to be a bit more generous with rate limits, but I'm now not sure - for the stuff I do limited context size would be a dealbreaker - basically lots and lots of documentation and guideline references, code review loops, documentation references and web searches, multi-repo exploration, lots of tool calls and so on.I might just upgrade to Anthropic's more expensive Max subscription or something to get more subsidized tokens.
sentences.zh: 在长时间的会话中，即使有工作流程和子代理，我通常会在 Claude Code 的上下文中达到大约 500-750k 代币（例如一次会话中的 4-16 小时工作，跨越多个 5 小时的限制）。我打算探索为 Codex 付费，因为 OpenAI 似乎对速率限制更慷慨一些，但我现在不确定 - 对于我做的东西，有限的上下文大小将是一个交易破坏者 - 基本上是大量的文档和指南参考、代码审查循环、文档参考和网络搜索、多存储库探索、大量工具调用等等。我可能只是升级到 Anthropic 更昂贵的 Max 订阅或其他东西以获得更多补贴代币。

sentences.en: Even with plan files and the plan mode, it's like a slot machine after compacting the context, sometimes steps or other details just evaporate in thin air, the less of that I need to do the better.
sentences.zh: 即使有了计划文件和计划模式，它就像压缩上下文后的老虎机一样，有时步骤或其他细节只是凭空消失，我需要做的越少越好。

sentences.en: It's not that it can't work, it just doesn't work reliably enough not to be annoying.Luckily DeepSeek V4 Pro, GLM 5.2 and Kimi K3 don't seem to have those limits either - though DS is around Sonnet, GLM 5.2 feels a bit above Sonnet and only K3 only really is in the proper Opus ballpark that's good enough for me to work undisturbed, even if it seems to be slower.
sentences.zh: 并不是说它不能工作，而是它工作不够可靠，不会让人烦恼。幸运的是，DeepSeek V4 Pro、GLM 5.2 和 Kimi K3 似乎也没有这些限制——尽管 DS 围绕 Sonnet，但 GLM 5.2 感觉有点高于 Sonnet，只有 K3 真正处于合适的 Opus 范围内，这足以让我不受干扰地工作，即使它看起来更慢。

blocks.html_en: I didn't try long coding sessions for a while. I had really noticeable performance degradation with all models above 100-150k and always try to stay below those numbers.

blocks.html_en:
Good move given some experienced issues and compaction across the 5.6 range is closer to 5.4 than 5.5, i.e solid and reliable.

Will say that 5.6-Sol is a minor bump in my benchmarks in most areas vs 5.5 but a severe regression in a few specific task focused on rearranging trees, addressing merge conflicts, etc. where the model to accomplish the task does not properly adhere to prompts in a way GPT-5 originally managed, not retaining parts of history in the way prompted despite specific instructions not to as that made the final completion easier…

I am of the conservative and cautious opinion that no model should be able to run destructive tasks at all, I have seen every model do things that make me concerned enough to maintain that opinion and know my evals can’t catch everything. But for 5.6-Sol specifically, I’d caution everyone to reevaluate how you run the model, maybe take a few more precautions you tend to forgo.

It is extremely capable as a reviewer and for extensive tasks, though for the later, the safety net I feel is required to be comfortable limits the utility. The code 5.6-Sol provides also still is a bit harder to parse in reviews.

Release strategy wise, feel it’s have been smarter to release only Luna and Sol now, then Terra a few weeks of posttraining later, I simply cannot see a purpose for it in the current form given how well both Luna and Sol scale up and down respectively with reasoning. Two models from a lab at a time is also the limit I feel one can properly assess at a time.

sentences.en: Good move given some experienced issues and compaction across the 5.6 range is closer to 5.4 than 5.5, i.e solid and reliable.Will say that 5.6-Sol is a minor bump in my benchmarks in most areas vs 5.5 but a severe regression in a few specific task focused on rearranging trees, addressing merge conflicts, etc. where the model to accomplish the task does not properly adhere to prompts in a way GPT-5 originally managed, not retaining parts of history in the way prompted despite specific instructions not to as that made the final completion easier…I am of the conservative and cautious opinion that no model should be able to run destructive tasks at all, I have seen every model do things that make me concerned enough to maintain that opinion and know my evals can’t catch everything.
sentences.zh: 考虑到一些有经验的问题，5.6 范围内的压缩比 5.5 更接近 5.4，即可靠可靠。会说 5.6-Sol 在大多数领域的基准测试中与 5.5 相比是一个小提升，但在一些专注于重新排列树、解决合并冲突等的特定任务中出现了严重的回归。其中完成任务的模型没有以 GPT-5 最初管理的方式正确遵循提示，没有以这种方式保留部分历史记录尽管有具体指示，但仍提示不要这样做，因为这使最终完成变得更容易……我持保守而谨慎的观点，认为任何模型都不应该能够运行破坏性任务，我看到每个模型所做的事情都让我足够关心以维持这种观点，并且知道我的评估无法捕捉到所有内容。

sentences.en: But for 5.6-Sol specifically, I’d caution everyone to reevaluate how you run the model, maybe take a few more precautions you tend to forgo.It is extremely capable as a reviewer and for extensive tasks, though for the later, the safety net I feel is required to be comfortable limits the utility.
sentences.zh: 但特别是对于 5.6-Sol，我会提醒每个人重新评估如何运行模型，也许采取更多您倾向于放弃的预防措施。它作为审阅者和广泛的任务非常有能力，尽管对于后者，我认为需要舒适的安全网限制了实用性。

sentences.en: The code 5.6-Sol provides also still is a bit harder to parse in reviews.Release strategy wise, feel it’s have been smarter to release only Luna and Sol now, then Terra a few weeks of posttraining later, I simply cannot see a purpose for it in the current form given how well both Luna and Sol scale up and down respectively with reasoning.
sentences.zh: 5.6-Sol 提供的代码在评论中仍然很难解析。就发布策略而言，感觉现在只发布 Luna 和 Sol 更明智，然后在训练后几周发布 Terra，鉴于 Luna 和 Sol 分别通过推理扩展和缩小规模，我根本看不到它在当前形式中的用途。

sentences.en: Two models from a lab at a time is also the limit I feel one can properly assess at a time.
sentences.zh: 一次来自实验室的两个模型也是我认为一次可以正确评估的极限。

Release strategy wise, feel it’s have been smarter to release only Luna and Sol now, then Terra a few weeks of posttraining later, I simply cannot see a purpose for it in the current form given how well both Luna and Sol scale up and down respectively with reasoning. Two models from a lab at a time is also the limit I feel one can properly assess at a time.

sentences.en: Good move given some experienced issues and compaction across the 5.6 range is closer to 5.4 than 5.5, i.e solid and reliable.Will say that 5.6-Sol is a minor bump in my benchmarks in most areas vs 5.5 but a severe regression in a few specific task focused on rearranging trees, addressing merge conflicts, etc. where the model to accomplish the task does not properly adhere to prompts in a way GPT-5 originally managed, not retaining parts of history in the way prompted despite specific instructions not to as that made the final completion easier…I am of the conservative and cautious opinion that no model should be able to run destructive tasks at all, I have seen every model do things that make me concerned enough to maintain that opinion and know my evals can’t catch everything.
sentences.zh: 考虑到一些有经验的问题，5.6 范围内的压缩比 5.5 更接近 5.4，即可靠可靠。会说 5.6-Sol 在大多数领域的基准测试中与 5.5 相比是一个小提升，但在一些专注于重新排列树、解决合并冲突等的特定任务中出现了严重的回归。其中完成任务的模型没有以 GPT-5 最初管理的方式正确遵循提示，没有以这种方式保留部分历史记录尽管有具体指示，但仍提示不要这样做，因为这使最终完成变得更容易……我持保守而谨慎的观点，认为任何模型都不应该能够运行破坏性任务，我看到每个模型所做的事情都让我足够关心以维持这种观点，并且知道我的评估无法捕捉到所有内容。

sentences.en: But for 5.6-Sol specifically, I’d caution everyone to reevaluate how you run the model, maybe take a few more precautions you tend to forgo.It is extremely capable as a reviewer and for extensive tasks, though for the later, the safety net I feel is required to be comfortable limits the utility.
sentences.zh: 但特别是对于 5.6-Sol，我会提醒每个人重新评估如何运行模型，也许采取更多您倾向于放弃的预防措施。它作为审阅者和广泛的任务非常有能力，尽管对于后者，我认为需要舒适的安全网限制了实用性。

sentences.en: The code 5.6-Sol provides also still is a bit harder to parse in reviews.Release strategy wise, feel it’s have been smarter to release only Luna and Sol now, then Terra a few weeks of posttraining later, I simply cannot see a purpose for it in the current form given how well both Luna and Sol scale up and down respectively with reasoning.
sentences.zh: 5.6-Sol 提供的代码在评论中仍然很难解析。就发布策略而言，感觉现在只发布 Luna 和 Sol 更明智，然后在训练后几周发布 Terra，鉴于 Luna 和 Sol 分别通过推理扩展和缩小规模，我根本看不到它在当前形式中的用途。

sentences.en: Two models from a lab at a time is also the limit I feel one can properly assess at a time.
sentences.zh: 一次来自实验室的两个模型也是我认为一次可以正确评估的极限。

blocks.html_en: Good move given some experienced issues and compaction across the 5.6 range is closer to 5.4 than 5.5, i.e solid and reliable.

sentences.zh: 考虑到一些有经验的问题，这是一个很好的举措，并且 5.6 范围内的压实度比 5.5 更接近 5.4，即坚固可靠。

But for 5.6-Sol specifically, I’d caution everyone to reevaluate how you run the model, maybe take a few more precautions you tend to forgo.

blocks.html_en:
With purely quadratic attention, the cost of the token at 372K is 87% more than the token at 272K.

There are attention mechanisms that help to mitigate this, but you can clearly see in the chart that Tibo posted that the attention mechanisms they are using are still scaling quadratically.

This becomes an optimization problem for token t:

min f[cost, quality loss] = compaction cost[t] + compaction quality loss[t] + token cost[t]

sentences.en: With purely quadratic attention, the cost of the token at 372K is 87% more than the token at 272K.There are attention mechanisms that help to mitigate this, but you can clearly see in the chart that Tibo posted that the attention mechanisms they are using are still scaling quadratically.This becomes an optimization problem for token t:min f[cost, quality loss] = compaction cost[t] + compaction quality loss[t] + token cost[t]
sentences.zh: 使用纯粹的二次注意力，372K 时的 token 成本比 272K 时的 token 多了 87%。有一些注意力机制可以帮助缓解这种情况，但你可以在 Tibo 发布的图表中清楚地看到，他们使用的注意力机制仍在二次缩放。这成为 token t 的优化问题：min f[cost,quality loss] = 压缩成本[t] + 压缩质量损失[t] + token cost[t]

blocks.html_en:
On the one hand, compacting at 300k saves OpenAI 40%. That's great.

On the other hand, $10 for 1M tokens still seems really high? It's not too hard to blow through that in an hour or two.

sentences.en: On the one hand, compacting at 300k saves OpenAI 40%.
sentences.zh: 一方面，压缩为 300k 可以为 OpenAI 节省 40%。

sentences.en: That's great.On the other hand, $10 for 1M tokens still seems really high?
sentences.zh: 太棒了。另一方面，100 万个代币 10 美元似乎仍然很高？

sentences.en: It's not too hard to blow through that in an hour or two.
sentences.zh: 一两个小时内完成这个任务并不难。

blocks.html_en:
You can run ’codex debug models' into jq!

I forget whether it's documented or not, and it is kind of annoying that that's how you find it, but it does tell you. Maybe useful for the future if nothing else.

blocks.html_en: This is a shame, and hopefully it reverts soon. I switched from Claude to Codex just before the Fable release because Claude doesn't follow instructions and goes off the ranch too frequently. I really don't want to have to switch back to what I consider to be a fundamentally worse model.

blocks.html_en:
Not sure if this is still up to date (2023), but https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.03172 shows that performance degrades mostly in the middle of the context.

Anecdotally I've been stuck in that situation of being at 400-500k tokens and "just one more prompt bro" will get the task done, and I appreciate not having to wait through a compaction. If anything, keeping the bloated context helps with accuracy at the expense of speed in these cases.

sentences.en: Not sure if this is still up to date (2023), but https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.03172 shows that performance degrades mostly in the middle of the context.Anecdotally I've been stuck in that situation of being at 400-500k tokens and "just one more prompt bro" will get the task done, and I appreciate not having to wait through a compaction.
sentences.zh: 不确定这是否仍然是最新的（2023 年），但 https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.03172 显示性能下降主要是在上下文中间。有趣的是，我一直陷入 400-500k 令牌的情况，“只需再一个提示兄弟”即可完成任务，我很高兴不必等待压缩。

sentences.en: If anything, keeping the bloated context helps with accuracy at the expense of speed in these cases.
sentences.zh: 如果有的话，在这些情况下，保持臃肿的上下文有助于提高准确性，但会牺牲速度。

blocks.html_en: I haven't used Codex in some time, but in Claude Code I force my context size down to 280k on purpose as in my experience your best work is done within the 30-250k~ range regardless of a models maximum context size.

sentences.en: I haven't used Codex in some time, but in Claude Code I force my context size down to 280k on purpose as in my experience your best work is done within the 30-250k~ range regardless of a models maximum context size.

sentences.zh: 我已经有一段时间没有使用 Codex 了，但在 Claude Code 中，我故意将上下文大小强制降低到 280k，因为根据我的经验，无论模型最大上下文大小如何，最好的工作都是在 30-250k~ 范围内完成。

blocks.html_en: I haven't used Codex in some time, but in Claude Code I force my context size down to 280k on purpose as in my experience your best work is done within the 30-250k~ range regardless of a models maximum context size.

sentences.zh: 我已经有一段时间没有使用 Codex 了，但在 Claude Code 中，我故意将上下文大小强制降低到 280k，因为根据我的经验，无论模型最大上下文大小如何，最好的工作都是在 30-250k~ 范围内完成。

blocks.html_en:
I have rule files that guides the agent towards my coding standards, code style, house rules etc. They alone cost 60-80k tokens, and they are the backbone of my system that prevents slop. Pre 1M context, I had to build complicated tooling to re-include the relevant docs to the context upon compaction, which relied on unstable transcription file format, which was a pain to maintain. With 1M context I deleted all of those. Nowadays most of my sessions uses 300-450k context.

Another thing that's preventing me from trying Codex. (the other is @ referencing files not auto including them to the context)

1M should be table stakes for frontier models at this point for programming.

sentences.en: I have rule files that guides the agent towards my coding standards, code style, house rules etc. They alone cost 60-80k tokens, and they are the backbone of my system that prevents slop.
sentences.zh: 我有规则文件，指导代理遵循我的编码标准、代码风格、内部规则等。仅它们就花费 60-80k 代币，它们是我的系统的支柱，可以防止溢出。

sentences.en: With 1M context I deleted all of those.
sentences.zh: 对于 1M 上下文，我删除了所有这些。

sentences.en: Nowadays most of my sessions uses 300-450k context.Another thing that's preventing me from trying Codex.
sentences.zh: 如今，我的大多数会话都使用 300-450k 上下文。另一件事阻碍了我尝试 Codex。

sentences.en: (the other is @ referencing files not auto including them to the context)1M should be table stakes for frontier models at this point for programming.
sentences.zh: （另一个是 @ 引用文件，不会自动将它们包含到上下文中）此时，1M 应该是前沿模型的赌注，用于编程。

blocks.html_en:
Try running your rule files through an LLM for optimization. 60k-80k tokens is massive.

Funnily enough, most anti-slop skills I found are both way too verbose and miss some common slop constructs.

I also reduced many rules from “When doing X, don’t do Y, but do Z.” Instead, the rule is “When doing X, do Z.” Fewer tokens and often works better.

I had one critical rule I was maintaining about searching the codebase using a structural index/graph and not grep. Every time the agent missed it, I asked it how to improve the rules. Eventually, I asked the AI to review that rule file and it rewrote it to be 30% smaller, but, crucially, structured to be more understandable by the LLM.

Another helpful thing was to ask AI to review my rules for things it can load on-demand when it works in that area.

sentences.en: Try running your rule files through an LLM for optimization.
sentences.zh: 尝试通过 LLM 运行您的规则文件以进行优化。

sentences.en: 60k-80k tokens is massive.Funnily enough, most anti-slop skills I found are both way too verbose and miss some common slop constructs.I also reduced many rules from “When doing X, don’t do Y, but do Z.”
sentences.zh: 60k-80k 令牌是巨大的。有趣的是，我发现的大多数反溢出技能都过于冗长，并且错过了一些常见的溢出结构。我还从“做 X 时，不做 Y，但做 Z”中减少了许多规则。

sentences.en: Instead, the rule is “When doing X, do Z.”
sentences.zh: 相反，规则是“做 X 时，做 Z”。

sentences.en: Fewer tokens and often works better.I had one critical rule I was maintaining about searching the codebase using a structural index/graph and not grep.
sentences.zh: 更少的标记通常效果更好。我有一个关于使用结构索引/图表而不是 grep 搜索代码库的关键规则。

sentences.en: Every time the agent missed it, I asked it how to improve the rules.
sentences.zh: 每次代理漏掉了，我就问它如何改进规则。

sentences.en: Eventually, I asked the AI to review that rule file and it rewrote it to be 30% smaller, but, crucially, structured to be more understandable by the LLM.Another helpful thing was to ask AI to review my rules for things it can load on-demand when it works in that area.
sentences.zh: 最终，我要求 AI 审查该规则文件，并将其重写为小 30%，但最重要的是，其结构更易于 LLM 理解。另一个有用的事情是要求 AI 审查我的规则，以了解它在该领域工作时可以按需加载的内容。

blocks.html_en:
It’s optimized to death. Every word, every comma. I have a rule file for writing rule files.

Problem stems from using my own abstractions instead of common frameworks.

For a full stack session, backend + front end + docs about the part we are working on adds to that amount.

I’ve accepted it because it results in code exactly the style I would’ve written. It’s a good tradeoff to cut off the slop.

sentences.en: It’s optimized to death.
sentences.zh: 它优化得要死。

sentences.en: Every word, every comma.
sentences.zh: 每个单词，每个逗号。

sentences.en: I have a rule file for writing rule files.Problem stems from using my own abstractions instead of common frameworks.For a full stack session, backend + front end + docs about the part we are working on adds to that amount.I’ve accepted it because it results in code exactly the style I would’ve written.
sentences.zh: 我有一个用于编写规则文件的规则文件。问题源于使用我自己的抽象而不是通用框架。对于完整的堆栈会话，后端+前端+有关我们正在处理的部分的文档增加了这个数量。我接受了它，因为它产生的代码与我编写的风格完全相同。

sentences.en: It’s a good tradeoff to cut off the slop.
sentences.zh: 切断斜坡是一个很好的权衡。

blocks.html_en:
AI cannot tell you why it did something. If you ask it why it didn't do X even though your prompt said so, it can come up with some plausible sounding explanation, which might even be correct, but there's a fundamental impedance mismatch between giving you the most plausible sounding answer and actually grounding that in knowledge of how it generated that answer.

TBF having a 60k personal style guide is also a bad idea, people get borderline AI-psychosed about how their special prompts are really steering the AI when in reality there is no way to evaluate this stuff. You invariably end up trying to define your style in terms of what it is and it isn't, it's like trying to define "red". "follow the style of my 5 most recent PRs" works pretty well.

sentences.en: AI cannot tell you why it did something.
sentences.zh: 人工智能无法告诉你它为什么做某事。

sentences.en: If you ask it why it didn't do X even though your prompt said so, it can come up with some plausible sounding explanation, which might even be correct, but there's a fundamental impedance mismatch between giving you the most plausible sounding answer and actually grounding that in knowledge of how it generated that answer.TBF having a 60k personal style guide is also a bad idea, people get borderline AI-psychosed about how their special prompts are really steering the AI when in reality there is no way to evaluate this stuff.
sentences.zh: 如果你问它为什么它没有做 X，即使你的提示是这么说的，它也会给出一些听起来合理的解释，甚至可能是正确的，但是在给你最合理的听起来答案和实际根据它如何生成该答案的知识之间存在根本的阻抗不匹配。TBF 有 60k 个人风格指南也是一个坏主意，人们对他们的特殊提示如何真正指导人工智能感到边缘人工智能，而实际上没有评价这个东西的方法。

sentences.en: You invariably end up trying to define your style in terms of what it is and it isn't, it's like trying to define "red".
sentences.zh: 你总是最终试图根据它是什么和不是什么来定义你的风格，就像试图定义“红色”一样。

sentences.en: "follow the style of my 5 most recent PRs" works pretty well.
sentences.zh: “遵循我最近 5 个 PR 的风格”效果很好。

sentences.zh: TBF 有一个 60k 的个人风格指南也是一个坏主意，人们对他们的特殊提示如何真正引导人工智能感到边缘人工智能心理，而实际上没有办法评估这些东西。

sentences.en: You invariably end up trying to define your style in terms of what it is and it isn't, it's like trying to define "red".
sentences.zh: 你总是最终试图根据它是什么和不是什么来定义你的风格，就像试图定义“红色”一样。

sentences.en: "follow the style of my 5 most recent PRs" works pretty well.
sentences.zh: “遵循我最近 5 个 PR 的风格”效果很好。

blocks.html_en:
Another interesting change in that commit is the addition of this section to the system prompt:

  Before taking a destructive action:
  
  - Make sure the action is clearly within
    the user's request.
  - Resolve the exact targets with
    read-only checks when necessary.
  - Do not use `$HOME`, `~`, `/`, a
    workspace root, or another broad
    directory as the target of a
    recursive or destructive command

Looks like the fix for this bug where Codex would occasionally accidentally delete your entire home directory: https://twitter.com/thsottiaux/status/2077630111499882637

sentences.zh: 该提交中另一个有趣的变化是在系统提示中添加了此部分： 在采取破坏性操作之前： - 确保该操作明确在用户的请求范围内。 - 必要时通过只读检查解决确切的目标。 - 不要使用 `$HOME`、`~`、`/`、工作区根目录或其他广泛目录作为递归或破坏性命令的目标看起来像是此错误的修复，其中 Codex 偶尔会意外删除整个主目录：https://twitter.com/thsottiaux/status/2077630111499882637

blocks.html_en:
https://github.com/tekacs/fast-rm

I've overridden my rm with this, which I threw together for fast-deletes of things like Rust target/ directories, and after seeing the GPT horror story, I taught it to flatly reject deletions directly under `/` and under home directories, with a message printing the path that it's trying to delete.

Not exactly a perfect mitigation, but given that the stated risk was the model mistakenly using the wrong $HOME, it seems like a reasonable safety. I should probably make it use an even scarier rejection notice, though.

I also... have backups.

sentences.zh: 不过，我可能应该使用更可怕的拒绝通知。我也......

sentences.en: have backups.
sentences.zh: 有备份。

blocks.html_en:
Codex is open source. You can build it yourself. You don't have to use upstream's parameter choices, compaction strategies, subagent heuristics, or whatever. Strange how people act like tweaks to these parameters are mandates when they're really just suggestions relayed to you via git.

For example: upstream recently changed the effort level hotkeys so M-. would stop at xhigh, not max. If you want max, upstream, you go to the /model menu. I didn't like this change, so I undid it locally. Easy peasy.

sentences.en: Codex is open source.
sentences.zh: Codex 是开源的。

sentences.en: You can build it yourself.
sentences.zh: 您可以自己构建它。

sentences.en: You don't have to use upstream's parameter choices, compaction strategies, subagent heuristics, or whatever.
sentences.zh: 您不必使用上游的参数选择、压缩策略、子代理启发法或其他任何东西。

sentences.en: Strange how people act like tweaks to these parameters are mandates when they're really just suggestions relayed to you via git.For example: upstream recently changed the effort level hotkeys so M-.
sentences.zh: 奇怪的是，人们对这些参数的调整似乎是强制性的，而实际上它们只是通过 git 转发给你的建议。例如：上游最近更改了工作级别热键，所以 M-。

sentences.en: would stop at xhigh, not max.
sentences.zh: 将停止在 xhigh，而不是 max。

sentences.en: If you want max, upstream, you go to the /model menu.
sentences.zh: 如果您想要 max、upstream，请转到 /model 菜单。

sentences.en: I didn't like this change, so I undid it locally.
sentences.zh: 我不喜欢这个改变，所以我在本地取消了它。

sentences.en: Easy peasy.
sentences.zh: 简单易行。

blocks.html_en:
Not much point making it closed source at this point, coding agent harnesses are trivial to reverse-engineer.

Being open source was also a useful community lever they could pull when they were trying to catch up with Claude Code.

blocks.html_en: Context size cuts like this are usually a cost/latency tradeoff rather than a capability one - serving a smaller window is cheaper and keeps latency in check, and most sessions probably don't need anywhere near 372k tokens anyway. Curious if this affects people who were actually relying on the larger window for big codebases.

sentences.en: Context size cuts like this are usually a cost/latency tradeoff rather than a capability one - serving a smaller window is cheaper and keeps latency in check, and most sessions probably don't need anywhere near 372k tokens anyway.
sentences.zh: 像这样的上下文大小削减通常是一种成本/延迟权衡，而不是能力权衡 - 提供较小的窗口更便宜并且可以控制延迟，而且大多数会话可能不需要接近 372k 令牌。

sentences.en: Curious if this affects people who were actually relying on the larger window for big codebases.
sentences.zh: 很好奇这是否会影响那些实际上依赖更大窗口来获取大型代码库的人。

content_html_zh: 我知道很多人喜欢说压缩使这个毫无意义，但不幸的是，对于我所做的大多数事情来说，压缩过程中丢失的细节水平太多了。也许如果你的计划没有那么多细节，或者如果你没有与很多细节进行讨论，那么没关系？缺乏长上下文是我最终仍然使用 Anthropic 的主要原因。最糟糕的是当你需要它来保存大量论文时在它的头脑中，或者需要完全分辨率的大型复杂材料中，你的上下文窗口最终会始终保持在 16%。 你有大约五分钟的对话，内容很紧凑，然后你必须等待它再次阅读，达到 16%... 和repeat.372并不完美，但它好多了，是天赐之物。 它把 12% 到 20% 变成了 40% 左右。一切都取决于上下文的连贯性。 例如。 在 Claude Code 的“1M context”模型中，我不愿意超过 30%。 因此，如果 Codex 272K 100% 一致，那么差异就较小。 尽管如此，“硬失败”的界限已经发生了变化。 运行时间较长的代理进程肯定会更频繁地遇到这种情况。事实上，没有办法禁用自动压缩，也没有办法在对话历史中返回压缩之前的状态，这使得 Codex 对我来说在任何大于 5kloc 的代码库上都是不行的。 更糟糕的是，它似乎在剩下 10-20% 的上下文时随机触发。 所以实际上 272k 中的 80% 是真正可用的上下文。 压缩会毁掉我的训练，它会产生幻觉，而且比重新开始更糟糕。 当我的计算机在大型代码库上烧毁令牌、达到 15%、自动压缩时，我已经有足够多的时间对我的计算机尖叫了，并且幻觉如此糟糕，以至于必须重新读取整个代码库，达到 15%，自动压缩......您是否尝试过使用其他线束（例如 Pi）？ 您可以轻松关闭自动压缩，如果您将其保留，您可以根据需要使用 /tree 返回压缩之前。> 你可以使用 /tree 在压缩之前返回，在我看来这确实是一个杀手级功能。 我目前正在开发 pi Brains 扩展，旨在解决我遇到的压缩问题，也就是说，我不知道压缩后人工智能知道什么。https://gitsense.com/screenshots/pi-inspect-overview.png 上面的屏幕截图包含我所说的压缩胶囊，其中包含压缩时的所有消息/事件。 右侧面板中的“文件”部分显示了自压缩以来读取/修改了多少文件。压缩后，我可以轻松地知道 Pi 使用什么来了解并使用 `/tree` 及时返回。在开始新会话之前，我仍然不会压缩超过 5 次，因为压缩消息仍然存在于 Pi 中。因此，实际的解决方法是将 model_auto_compact_token_limit 设置为非常接近模型的上下文窗口，但仍低于模型的上下文窗口。 这会推迟压缩而不是禁用 it.config.toml model_context_window = 27000 model_auto_compact_token_limit = 17000 model_auto_compact_token_limit_scope = "total"> 事实上，没有办法禁用自动压缩，也没有办法在对话历史记录中返回到压缩之前，这使得 Codex 对 meSame 来说是不行的。 我已经多次向 @tibo 讲述过这些问题，但显然 OpenAI 的重点是尽快为他们带来最多用户的功能，而不是提高线束质量。也许不是对着你的电脑大喊大叫，而是想出一个更好的上下文管理策略？我确实找到了一个策略，非常感谢，而且很简单： npm uninstall -g @openai/codex && curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh |这是最后一根稻草，促使我降级 OpenAI 订阅，并将大部分支出转移到 Anthropic 和 Claude 代码上。OpenAI 开发人员，如果您正在阅读，这就是您失去客户的方式。所以你从一个糟糕的产品变成了一个更糟糕的产品？ 那里有很好的解决方案，但你会说“不，不需要一个好的解决方案。 我想要一个更糟糕的解决方案。”^ 这就是操作人员所做的。 不要标记事实。克劳德也做同样的事情？它要求你创建新的聊天（克劳德设计），克劳德代码也会促使你重新开始，但我认为“/compact”是可选的？当你达到上下文限制时，Claude 也会做同样的事情，但不同的是 claude 的上下文限制是 1m，而 Codex 现在是 272k。 克劳德让您可以完成更多工作，而不必担心开始时间会被压缩。您是否观察到在高于 200k 的上下文中性能有所下降？这曾经是为什么在上下文接近 150 到 170k 时通常首选清除或压缩上下文的主要问题之一。我最近看到它超过 300-350k，但即使超过 250k，跌幅也可能较小。Opus：是的，在 600-700k 时可能会有点模糊，一般来说我更喜欢 400K 及以下。 但是，我已经有 850k 的可用会话。这实际上取决于会话，以及您正在做什么，您可以将其推进多远。事实并非如此，在我现在正在进行的项目中，大部分较长的提示似乎消耗了 300k 到 400k 的令牌（输入 + 输出），而且我发现 Opus 4.8 和 Fable 5 似乎并没有因为那么多上下文而迷失。 至少结果对我来说看起来仍然不错。不过，我认为我从未突破过 500k，但在官方支持的上限时，它很可能会崩溃。这似乎与我的设计过程完全不同。 我写了一个 plan.md，经过多次修改。 计划就是记忆。 重新启动并再次阅读计划以进行另一次审查是获得不同观点的好方法。我喜欢实际上有分层计划。从大想法开始。 确定产品经理级别的描述，也许还有一些关于哪些功能已加入或取消的细节，分阶段设计/路线图将如何工作，也许基本技术堆栈是什么。该文档被保存，并具有大背景。接下来，我们更深入一些，充实模式、API，更多关于代码应该是什么样子的，整体测试策略。 该文档被保存，人工智能可以参考它的上下文。下一个级别是一个非常具体的实施计划。 想象一下史诗中的个人小 JIRA 门票。 AI 根据之前的上下文编写此内容。 我确保每个单独的步骤都有关于分支名称、哪个分支从哪里分支、该步骤将完成的测试和文档的非常明确的说明。 从更高级别的文档中这一点应该是显而易见的，但这有助于让人工智能只关心其上下文窗口中的本地文档。然后有一个为子代理设计的堆叠拉取请求的 DAG，以供子代理处理。所有这些都使上下文窗口保持较小（以及成本下降和准确性提高）并且工作得非常好，并且我可以获得大量易于​​通过堆叠 PR 进行审查的代码。 我总是想要修复早期的问题，然后将其渗透到堆栈中，所以我有一些这方面的技能。我尝试做类似的事情。 我什至会将计划的各个部分分解为单独的文档，并根据需要使用 /clear 来一一引用它们。 这是一个更加手动的过程，但还不是最糟糕的。 我什至可能会掌握某种技能，通过子代理或类似的工具将其自动化。您可以告诉您的主要代理“我在 <DIRECTORY>
